scenario: Quanta SaaS provisions 1500 tenants per day in Camunda 8.8 by iterating a multi-instance service task over a tenant list. The platform engineer reviews how the multi-instance body relates to the inner activity so workers are scheduled correctly.
question: What does the multi-instance body do at activation time?

options.text: The multi-instance body activates and creates one inner activity instance for every element of the inputCollection, either sequentially or in parallel
The multi-instance body activates and creates a single inner activity instance that is then re-entered once for each element of the inputCollection in turn
The multi-instance body activates and waits for an external job worker to publish each element of the inputCollection before any inner activity instance is created
The multi-instance body activates and creates one inner activity instance per partition of the running Zeebe cluster, independent of the inputCollection size

a.text: Correct. The documentation states that when the activity is entered, the multi-instance body is activated and one instance for every element of the inputCollection is created, either sequentially or in parallel.

b.text: Incorrect. The documented behavior is to create one instance per element rather than re-entering a single instance; re-entry semantics are not part of the multi-instance body description.

c.text: Incorrect. The multi-instance body evaluates the inputCollection expression locally on activation; it does not subscribe to a job worker to obtain the collection elements one at a time.

d.text: Incorrect. The number of instances is driven by the size of the inputCollection, not by the number of partitions in the running Zeebe cluster as suggested by the option.

explanation: The multi-instance body activates and creates one inner activity instance per element of the inputCollection, with the chosen execution mode determining whether instances run sequentially or in parallel.
